lede: A look at February 23 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including George Frideric Handel and Casimir Funk, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: February 2021 Ecuadorian prison riots
events.desc: Four simultaneous prison riots leave at least 62 people dead in Ecuador.

events.title: SN 1987A
events.desc: Supernova 1987a is seen in the Large Magellanic Cloud.

events.title: Cavan Orphanage fire
events.desc: The Cavan Orphanage fire kills thirty-five girls and an elderly cook.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Japanese submarines fire artillery shells at the coastline near Santa Barbara, California.

events.title: Plutonium
events.desc: Plutonium is first produced and isolated by Dr. Glenn T. Seaborg.

events.title: Charles Martin Hall
events.desc: Charles Martin Hall produced the first samples of aluminium from the electrolysis of aluminium oxide, after several years of intensive work. He was assisted in this project by his older sister, Julia Brainerd Hall.

events.title: Abraham Lincoln
events.desc: President-elect Abraham Lincoln arrives secretly in Washington, D.C., after the thwarting of an alleged assassination plot in Baltimore, Maryland.

events.title: Mexican–American War
events.desc: Mexican–American War: Battle of Buena Vista: In Mexico, American troops under future president General Zachary Taylor defeat Mexican General Antonio López de Santa Anna.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: Baron von Steuben arrives at Valley Forge, Pennsylvania, to help train the Continental Army.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: J. S. Bach leads his Tafel-Music Shepherd Cantata for the birthday of Christian, Duke of Saxe-Weissenfels.

births.name: Jean-Baptiste Le Moyne de Bienville
births.line: Sieur de Bienville, Canadian politician, 2nd Colonial Governor of Louisiana

births.name: Radosław Sikorski
births.line: Polish journalist and politician, 11th Minister of Foreign Affairs of Poland

deaths.text: Carl Friedrich Gauss, German mathematician, astronomer, and physicist
John Quincy Adams, American politician, 6th President of the United States
Emperor Yingzong of Ming, Emperor of China (1435–1449, 1457–1464)

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Rafaela Ybarra de Vilallonga
The Emperor's Birthday, birthday of Naruhito, the current Emperor of Japan (Japan)
